title: humboltia tenuissima
intro: Humboldtia tenuissima is a plant species commonly known as the 'Corkwood Tree' or 'Katu', which is native to the tropical regions of India and Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its bark and leaves, which contain compounds with anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Humboldtia tenuissima include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, skin infections, and respiratory issues. Traditional herbal preparations of the plant involve using its bark to make a decoction, which is often used to treat various ailments.
